title: Droplet ecosystem
abstract: Disclosed herein are droplet ecosystems comprising a producer organism and a recipient organism, wherein the producer organism and recipient organism are encapsulated in a water-in-oil droplet, wherein the producer organism produces one or more molecules that interact with the recipient organism in the same ecosystem, and wherein the producer organism is distinct from the recipient organism. Also provided herein are methods of making and using the droplet ecosystems.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A droplet ecosystem comprising: a producer organism; and a recipient organism, wherein the producer organism and recipient organism are encapsulated in a droplet, wherein the producer organism produces one or more molecules that interact with the recipient organism in the same ecosystem, and wherein the producer organism is distinct from the recipient organism. 2. The droplet ecosystem of claim 1, wherein the droplet is a water-in-oil droplet. 3. The droplet ecosystem of any of claims 1-2, wherein the interaction results in an observable phenotype in the recipient. 4. The droplet ecosystem of any of claims 1-3, wherein the producer organism is a phage producing bacteria and the recipient organism is a mammalian cell. 5. The droplet ecosystem of any of claims 1-4, wherein the producer organism produces phages displaying an agonist antibody. 6. The droplet ecosystem of claim 5, wherein the agonist antibody interacts with the mammalian cell in the same droplet. 7. The droplet ecosystem of claim 6, wherein the interaction results in an observable phenotype in the mammalian cell. 8. The droplet ecosystem of any of claims 1-7, wherein the producer organism is yeast and the recipient organism is a mammalian cell. 9. The droplet ecosystem of claim 8, wherein the yeast produces a polypeptide candidate for selection by the mammalian cell. 10. The droplet ecosystem of any of claims 1-9, wherein the droplet ecosystem is for selecting functional polypeptides for drug discovery. 11. The droplet ecosystem of claim 1, wherein the producer organism and the recipient organisms are two distinct mammalian cells derived from two different organisms. 12. The droplet ecosystem of claim 10, wherein the droplet ecosystem is for analyzing secretomes. 13. The droplet ecosystem of any of claims 1-12, wherein the producer organism is a bacterial cell, a mammalian cell, and/or yeast. 14. The droplet ecosystem of any of claims 1-14, wherein the recipient organism is a bacterial cell, a mammalian cell, and/or yeast. 15. The droplet ecosystem of any of claims 1-14, wherein the droplet is a picoliter sized droplet. 16. The droplet ecosystem of any of claims 1-15, wherein the recipient organism comprises a reporter system, which reports when the producer organism interacts with the recipient organism. 17. The droplet ecosystem of claim 16, wherein the reporter system comprises expression of a fluorescence protein on the cell surface of the recipient organism when the recipient organism is activated by an agonist produced by the producer organism. 18. A method of making a droplet ecosystem comprising two distinct organisms encapsulated in a water-in-oil droplet, comprising: combining a culture of the first organism with a culture of the second organism and an oil. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein the oil is a fluorinated oil. 20. The method of any of claims 18-19, wherein the combining step is by vortexing a mixture of the first organism, the second organism, and the fluorinated oil. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the combining step is by using a microfluidic device. 22. A method of paracrine based selection of a therapeutic antibody, comprising: providing a selection platform comprising a phage-producing bacteria and a mammalian cell encapsulated in a water-in-oil droplet; analyzing the phenotype of mammalian cells in response to a phage displaying an agonist antibody produced by the bacteria; and selecting a therapeutic antibody by selecting those droplets in which the mammalian cell displays a phenotype of interest. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the droplet is a picoliter sized droplet. 24. The method of any of claims 22-23, wherein each droplet is a mini-ecosystem in which the bacteria produce phage displaying a unique antibody that only interact with the mammalian cells in the same droplet. 25. The method of any of claims 22-24, wherein the therapeutic antibody is selected by analyzing each droplet individually using a FACS instrument. 26. The method of any of claims 22-25, wherein the selection platform comprises at least 10 2 , 10 3 , 10 4 , 10 5 , 10 6 , 10 7 , 10 8 , 10 9 , 10 10 or more water-in-oil droplets comprising the phage- producing bacteria and the mammalian cell. 27. A droplet ecosystem comprising: a DNA encoded chemical library; and a mammalian cell, wherein the DNA encoded chemical library and the mammalian cell are encapsulated in a droplet. 28. The droplet ecosystem of claim 27, wherein there is a change in phenotype of the mammalian cell when a compound in the chemical library interacts with the mammalian cell. 29. The droplet ecosystem of any of claims 27-28, wherein the ecosystem is for screening the DNA-encoded chemical library for drug discovery. 30. A method of optimizing reaction conditions between a first reactant and a second reactant, comprising: providing a reaction mixture of the first reactant and the second reactant; dividing the reaction mixture into femtoliter sized compartments; and varying the reaction conditions in the femtoliter sized compartments to optimize reaction conditions. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein varying the reaction conditions comprise varying the nature of the solvent in the femtoliter sized compartments. 32. The method of any of claims 30-31, wherein varying the reaction conditions comprise varying the temperature and/or pressure in the femtoliter sized compartments.
